1: A revelation of God: "Blessed is everyone who fears the LORD, who walks in his ways." Proverbs 1:7 — the fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom. Christ is the wisdom of God incarnate (1 Corinthians 1:30 — "Christ Jesus, who became to us wisdom from God"). He perfectly "walks in God's ways" (John 8:29 — "I always do the things that are pleasing to him") and shares the blessedness of the God-fearer with all who are in him.
2: A theme: "You shall eat the fruit of the labor of your hands; you shall be blessed, and it shall be well with you." The fruitfulness belongs to those who abide in Christ. John 15:5 — "Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing." The laborer who eats the fruit of fruitful work is the believer whose labor in Christ is never in vain (1 Corinthians 15:58).
3: A theme: "Your wife will be like a fruitful vine within your house; your children will be like olive shoots around your table." The covenantal household bears fruit through Christ. Revelation 21:2,9 — the church is the bride of Christ; the household of God (Ephesians 2:19) gathers around the table of the Lord (1 Corinthians 11:23-26). The fruitfulness of the vine is realized in Christ the true vine (John 15:1-8).
4: A revelation of God: the principle restated — "thus shall the man be blessed who fears the LORD." The blessing flows from right relationship with God, which is now mediated through Christ. Acts 3:25-26 — God raised up his servant and "sent him to you first, to bless you by turning every one of you from your wickedness." The ancient blessing given to Abraham comes to all peoples through Christ.
5: A shadow: "The LORD bless you from Zion! May you see the prosperity of Jerusalem all the days of your life!" The blessing that flows from Zion flows from the heavenly Jerusalem. Hebrews 12:22-24 — "you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem... to Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant." The prosperity of Jerusalem is the shalom of God's eternal city, centered on Christ.
6: A theme: "May you see your children's children! Peace be upon Israel!" The multigenerational vision is the vision of the church that Christ builds across all ages and nations. Matthew 16:18 — "I will build my church, and the gates of hell shall not prevail against it." The "peace upon Israel" is the peace Christ himself is (Ephesians 2:14), declared over a community that includes every nation (Galatians 3:29).
